title: TC001-Student can log in and reach student dashboard
description: Verifies a SISWA user can log in with valid credentials and is redirected to the student dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Login').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/siswa' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Dashboard').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Admin can log in and reach admin dashboard
description: Verifies an ADMIN user can log in with valid credentials and is redirected to the admin dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type admin@yomu.id into the email input (index 6), then type admin123 into the password input (index 7), then click the login button (index 11).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin@yomu.id')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Login').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/admin' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login failed - error message 'Failed to fetch' is displayed on the login page after submitting credentials
- Admin dashboard not reached - current URL remains '/login' and does not contain '/admin'
- No redirect to '/admin' occurred within the observation period after submitting the login form

title: TC003-Successful student registration redirects to login
description: Verifies a new student can register with valid profile details and is redirected to the login page with a visible success confirmation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Daftar sebagai Siswa →' link (element index 90) to navigate to the registration page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /register (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:3001/register) to reach the registration page, then proceed with the registration assertions and form filling.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/register", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the registration form with the provided valid details and submit: enter email, name, select Class 10 and Science, enter matching password and confirm, then click 'Daftar Sekarang'. After submission verify redirect to /login and success message.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('test.student.unique.20260308@yomu-example.test')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Test Student')
        
        # -> Open the Kelas (Class) dropdown to reveal options so 'Class 10' can be selected (click element index 463).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div[3]/div/div/div/select').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Select 'Class 10' from the Kelas dropdown (index 463) and then continue to select major, fill passwords and submit the form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div[4]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('P@ssw0rd123!')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Register').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/login' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Registration successful').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Class dropdown on /register contains only placeholder 'Pilih Kelas' and does not include 'Class 10'
- Major dropdown on /register contains only placeholder 'Pilih Jurusan' and does not include 'Science'
- Registration cannot be completed because required selectable options are missing from the form
- Page title does not contain 'Register' (it shows localized 'Daftar Anggota Baru'), so the expected title check fails
- Registration submission was not performed; confirm-password and submit steps remain unexecuted because of missing fields

title: TC009-Forgot Password - request reset with registered email shows success confirmation
description: Verifies that submitting a registered email on Forgot Password shows a visible success message indicating a reset email was sent.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Lupa password?' link (element index 77) to open the forgot password page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/label/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /forgot-password so the 'Forgot Password' page can be validated (then verify the 'Forgot Password' text is visible)
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/forgot-password", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type admin@yomu.id into the email field (element 288), then submit the form by clicking the submit button (element 348).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin@yomu.id')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Forgot Password').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Password reset email sent').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div/input').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- No visible success/confirmation message displayed after submitting the registered email 'admin@yomu.id'.
- Visible page text contains only the forgot-password form and instructional lines; no expected confirmation phrases (e.g., 'telah dikirim', 'periksa email', 'check your email', 'email sent').
- Submit button showed an in-progress state but no final success notification was displayed.
- The email input field remained visible after submit, indicating the page did not present a dedicated confirmation view.

title: TC010-Forgot Password - unknown email shows email-not-found error
description: Verifies that submitting an unregistered email displays a visible error message (email not found).
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Lupa password?' link to navigate to the forgot-password page (use element index 77).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/label/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate explicitly to http://localhost:3001/forgot-password (user requested navigation to this path).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/forgot-password", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Verify the forgot-password heading is visible (ASSERTION) then enter the unregistered email into the email input and submit the form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('unknown-user-404@example.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Forgot Password').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Email not found').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- No error message displayed after submitting unregistered email 'unknown-user-404@example.com'.
- Expected error text indicating the email was not found is not present on the page.
- Notification area (section aria-label=Notifications) contains no visible error content after submit.

title: TC019-Student dashboard loads and shows statistics and recommendations
description: Verifies an authenticated student can open the dashboard and see stats and recommended books.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the provided username into the email field (index 6), then type the password into the password field (index 7), then click the Login button (index 11).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin@yomu.id')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Retry clicking the Login button to reattempt authentication, then wait for the page to respond and check for redirect to '/siswa'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/siswa' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Dashboard').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//section[contains(., "Statistics")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//section[contains(., "Recommended Books")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login failed - error banner 'Failed to fetch' displayed after submitting credentials.
- No redirect to '/siswa' observed; the application remained on the login page.
- Dashboard page did not load; expected 'Dashboard' content is not present.
- Authentication endpoint appears unreachable (network fetch error) preventing login.

title: TC020-Open a recommended book from dashboard to reach the book detail page
description: Verifies a student can click a recommended book card from the dashboard and land on the book detail page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields and click the 'Masuk ke Akun' (Login) button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin@yomu.id')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Retry clicking the 'Masuk ke Akun' (Login) button to attempt login again, then wait for the app to respond and redirect to the dashboard.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/form/button').nth(0)

async def run_test():

            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode

        # Input text

        frame = context.pages[-1]
